summaries.103464: Confirmed reproducible on Windows stable (2.10.5) and master by a triager. A comment pinpoints the root cause to the `accessibility_bridge_delegate_windows.cc` ignoring `DOCUMENT_SELECTION_CHANGED` events and suggests `EVENT_OBJECT_TEXTSELECTIONCHANGED` as the correct Windows event. NVDA is identified as the correct screen reader for testing; Narrator is misleading. One commenter notes NVDA still reads entire field contents on every change as of 2023.1. The issue has been open for over 3 years with no fix; Windows team added it to Q4 planning but no PR has landed and the issue is stalled.
summaries.122322: Root cause is that the local_auth Windows plugin opens a UAC-style biometric dialog (via `IUserConsentVerifierInterop::RequestVerificationForWindowAsync`) which takes focus away from the Flutter window without restoring it. A commenter proposes calling `SetFocus(child_content_)` or sending `WM_ACTIVATE` after authentication completes. Workaround using the `window_manager` package (blur, focus, setAlwaysOnTop toggle) is confirmed working by multiple users. No official fix has been merged; issue is stalled waiting on the Windows team or local_auth plugin owners.
summaries.81314: Confirmed reproducible on Android with `TextInputType.number` and Gboard with `autofocus: true`; the bug does not reproduce with SwiftKey or without autofocus. The root cause is that Gboard sends `oldValue.selection` already offset by one before the backspace is processed, causing `formatEditUpdate` to compute an incorrect cursor position. Confirmed on Android 11 and emulators. The issue was assigned to @Renzo-Olivares but the assignee was subsequently removed due to inactivity; currently stalled.
summaries.100455: Comments confirm the bug is reproducible on both stable (2.10.3) and master on macOS web (Chrome). The root cause is that macOS remaps the logical key for modifier+digit combinations (e.g., cmd+1 produces `¡` for option+1 release), so Flutter's `_pressedKeys` table records the wrong logical key and the assertion fires on key-up. Additional commenters report the same assertion with Korean IME (space/shift trigger it), Windows Emoji picker (web on Windows), and Apple Magic Keyboard with no special key combos. One commenter notes the exception renders the keyboard stuck. No fix or PR mentioned; issue is stalled and open.
summaries.86668: Confirmed reproducible on stable and master by a triager (video provided); trailing spaces do not push text or break lines as expected when `textAlign` is `TextAlign.center` or `TextAlign.right`. An initial comment wrongly suggested the behavior was correct; subsequent comments and a video confirmed it is not. A bot comment noted a missing priority label. No root cause was identified in the comments and no fix or PR was proposed; issue is stalled.
summaries.99653: The bug is confirmed on Windows with Microsoft Pinyin/Japanese IME: pressing Right Shift (or Left Shift in some configurations) triggers a `KeyUpEvent` assertion because the IME absorbs the key-down and only emits the key-up, causing `_pressedKeys` to have no record of the physical key. Multiple commenters confirm it affects Windows 10 (pre-19042 and later) and Windows 11 with Microsoft IMEs but not Sougou IME or Windows Server 2022. One user reports it was introduced in Flutter 3.3 and not present in 3.0.5, and that the Flutter 3.4 beta appeared fixed. The issue is referenced alongside #99465. Currently stalled with no merged fix.
summaries.58877: Comments confirm the issue is reproducible through Flutter 3.7 on iOS simulator and Android inside a `showModalBottomSheet` + `SingleChildScrollView`; calling `requestFocus` on a node that already has focus does not trigger auto-scroll. The root cause identified is that when the focus node already reports `hasFocus`, Flutter skips the scroll-into-view path. A workaround using a `ScrollController.animateTo(0)` before `requestFocus` is documented in the thread. The issue remains open on stable 3.3 and master 3.7 per a 2023 comment with a fresh video.
summaries.66588: Comments show the issue is not consistently reproducible: one triager could not reproduce it on master while the reporter could. One commenter explains the likely cause is a "stuck key" (a key held down during focus switch leaves `_pressedKeys` in an inconsistent state). Another commenter clarifies that a related issue is a usage mistake requiring `Shortcuts` wrapping when multiple focus nodes are present, pointing to #109912. The issue is stalled with no fix and labeled as a potential false positive for some sub-cases.
